quick_facts.label: Recruiting route
quick_facts.value: Junior Account Assistant cum Typist (JAACT) is a graduate-level Non-Technical Popular Categories post recruited through Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs), as illustrated by Centralised Employment Notice (CEN) 05/2024.

quick_facts.label: Minimum qualification
quick_facts.value: In CEN 05/2024, the post required a degree from a recognised university or equivalent, plus typing proficiency in English or Hindi on computer.

quick_facts.label: Selection stages
quick_facts.value: The cited RRB NTPC graduate notice specified two computer-based tests, followed for JAACT candidates by a Typing Skill Test; document verification and medical examination apply before appointment.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: CEN 05/2024 listed JAACT at Pay Level 5 of the 7th CPC pay matrix, with initial basic pay of ₹29,200. This is a dated recruitment-notice figure, not a guaranteed current gross or take-home salary.

quick_facts.label: Medical standard
quick_facts.value: The CEN 05/2024 post-parameter table listed medical standard C-2 for JAACT; candidates must meet the medical requirements in the applicable notice.

sections.heading: What the role supports
paragraphs: JAACT is an Accounts Department clerical role. Indian Railways’ Accounts Code describes railway accounts-office work as spanning booking and compilation, budget, cash and pay, compensation claims, engineering accounts, and other financial functions. In that environment, a junior accounts assistant can be expected to support the accurate recording, compilation, checking, retrieval, and typed processing of financial papers and records under local allocation of work; the exact desk duties vary by railway, division, production unit, and accounts branch.

The control environment is consequential: Railway accounts procedures require internal checks over cash and pay work, vouchers, receipts, payments, and related records. This makes careful data handling, document control, arithmetic accuracy, and adherence to prescribed procedures central to the work rather than merely administrative extras.
bullets: Maintain and process accounts-office records, statements, vouchers, bills, and correspondence as assigned.
Assist with compilation and checking work connected with railway receipts, expenditure, payroll/cash-and-pay, budget, or other accounts branches.
Type and prepare official material on computer, while following applicable financial-code, office-procedure, and internal-check requirements.

sections.heading: Entry through RRB NTPC
paragraphs: The principal open-market route is an RRB NTPC graduate-level notification that includes JAACT vacancies. In CEN 05/2024, applicants needed a recognised university degree or equivalent and computer typing proficiency in English or Hindi. Eligibility, age limits, reservation, medical standards, vacancy distribution, and application dates are notification-specific and must be checked in the live CEN for the relevant recruitment cycle.

For the CEN 05/2024 process, the RRB prescribed CBT 1 and CBT 2, then a qualifying Typing Skill Test for JAACT, followed by document verification and medical examination. The notice states that typing-test marks are not added to merit; candidates need 30 words per minute in English or 25 words per minute in Hindi on a computer without editing tools or spell-check facilities.

Indian Railway Establishment Manual material also records an internal departmental path into the historical Junior Accounts Assistant grade from Accounts Clerk roles. That route is governed by departmental quotas, service conditions, examinations, and current railway instructions, so it should not be treated as a substitute for the RRB direct-recruitment route.
bullets: Monitor official RRB websites for a current NTPC graduate CEN and its RRB-wise vacancy table.
Confirm degree status, age and category eligibility, C-2 medical standard, and required documents against that specific notification.
Prepare for both CBT stages and practise computer typing in the permitted English or Hindi mode.
Treat departmental promotion as an internal-service route subject to extant railway rules, not as an external application pathway.

sections.heading: Skills to build
paragraphs: The post’s formal recruitment requirements make computer typing and dependable digital work essential. RRB’s CEN 05/2024 required English/Hindi computer typing proficiency and used a qualifying typing test for this post. Candidates should therefore build sustained speed with accuracy, comfort working on a standard desktop keyboard, and the ability to produce clean official text without relying on spell-check or editing tools during the test.

The work context also rewards practical accounting discipline: numerical accuracy, reconciliation-minded checking, orderly handling of vouchers and files, spreadsheet and office-software fluency, confidentiality, and the ability to apply written rules. These are role-relevant capabilities inferred from the documented accounts-office functions and internal-control requirements, rather than a separate published JAACT competency framework.
bullets: English or Hindi computer typing, with accuracy as well as speed.
Numeracy, basic accounting concepts, and careful checking of entries and supporting documents.
Spreadsheets, word processing, structured digital records, and file management.
Reading and applying financial/administrative procedures; attention to audit trails and confidentiality.

sections.heading: Where the job is based
paragraphs: The CEN 05/2024 notice advertised JAACT vacancies in Zonal Railways and Production Units of Indian Railways. Actual posting depends on the RRB, notified railway/unit, vacancy allocation, medical fitness, and administrative requirements; applicants should not assume a preferred city or home-state posting.

The usual setting is an accounts office or related railway administrative unit. Railway Accounts Code material identifies accounts-office divisions including budget, booking and compilation, cash and pay, compensation claims, and engineering accounts, indicating that the role may sit in different finance-and-records workflows rather than in one uniform desk assignment.
bullets: Potential employers/locations: Zonal Railways and Production Units notified through the selected RRB.
Typical setting: office-based accounts, finance, cash-and-pay, budget, compilation, or related administrative work.
Posting and branch allocation are variable and governed by the recruitment notice and railway administration.

sections.heading: Career development context
paragraphs: The Indian Railway Establishment Manual sets out an accounts-cadre progression framework in which Junior Accounts Assistants could progress to Accounts Assistant after three years of service and passing the Appendix II examination; it also describes further channels involving Section Officer (Accounts), Inspector of Stores Accounts, and Travelling Inspector of Accounts, subject to qualifying examinations and the applicable rules. The cited manual uses legacy pay-scale terminology, so it is useful for understanding the broad cadre pathway but not for predicting current pay levels, vacancies, timelines, or promotion entitlement.

Progression in practice depends on the sanctioned cadre, seniority, fitness/selection process where applicable, qualifying departmental examinations, and extant Railway Board instructions. A new JAACT should confirm the current divisional or zonal Accounts Department promotion rules after joining.
bullets: Build mastery of railway accounts procedures and departmental examinations required for the assigned cadre path.
Track current seniority, eligibility, selection, and training rules through the employing railway rather than relying on historical pay-scale references.

Possible higher accounts functions described in the manual include Accounts Assistant and, for qualified staff, Section Officer (Accounts), Inspector of Stores Accounts, or Travelling Inspector of Accounts.

sections.heading: Pay and compensation context
paragraphs: For dated context, the official CEN 05/2024 indicative notice placed JAACT in Level 5 under the 7th CPC pay matrix and stated an initial basic pay of ₹29,200. That number is basic pay at the time and terms of that notice, not an all-inclusive monthly salary.

Actual gross pay and take-home pay vary with the prevailing dearness allowance, house-rent allowance where admissible, transport and other eligible allowances, place of posting, deductions, and changes to government pay rules. Candidates should use the current appointment offer, railway payroll guidance, and applicable government orders for a current figure.
bullets: Dated official notice context: Pay Level 5, initial basic pay ₹29,200 under CEN 05/2024.
Do not equate initial basic pay with take-home pay.
Allowance and deduction outcomes are posting- and rule-dependent and can change over time.
